titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jean O'Leary Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:22

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of professional papers, correspondence, personal papers, photographs, and computer and audiovisual media by or relating to gay rights activist and former Catholic nun Jean O'Leary.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 19 (computer media): Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 20 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Professional Papers, 1973-2005, undated. II. Correspondence, 1976-2004, undated. III. Personal Papers, 1963-2005. IV. Photographs, circa 1970-2005. V. Computer and Audiovisual Media, circa 1990-1994, undated.

bioghist.head: Jean O'Leary (1948-2005)
bioghist.p: Jean O'Leary was a former Roman Catholic nun who left her convent in 1971 to join the gay rights movement in New York City. O'Leary was a member of the Gay Activists Alliance until 1972 when she broke away from the group and formed Lesbian Feminist Liberation. She was co-executive director of the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force from 1976 to 1979 and subsequently became executive director of National Gay Rights Advocates. In 1977 O'Leary and Midge Costanza, her then-partner and special assistant to President Jimmy Carter, organized the first meeting of gay rights activists at the White House. O'Leary was also a speaker at the 1977 National Women's Conference. In 1988, she co-founded National Coming Out Day. O'Leary was active in Democratic Party politics and chaired the Democratic National Committee's Gay and Lesbian Caucus from 1992 to 2002; her marketing company, O'Leary & Associates, worked on political campaigns in California for Barbara Boxer, Ron Brown, and Dianne Feinstein. She and her partner, Lisa Phelps, had a daughter, Victoria, and a son David de Maria.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jean O'Leary Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization. Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of professional papers, correspondence, personal papers, photographs, and computer and audiovisual media by or relating to gay rights activist and former Catholic nun Jean O'Leary. Professional papers document her work as executive director of National Gay Rights Advocates, including a lawsuit against the National Institutes of Health and Food and Drug Administration over approval times for AIDS drugs. Also present are materials related to the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force, National Coming Out Day, Gay Activists Alliance, Access Now for Gay and Lesbian Equality, and the Democratic National Committee, and O'Leary's writings and speeches concerning advocacy and legal rights of gays and lesbians. Among the correspondents are Barbara Boxer, Ron Brown, Bill Clinton, Midge Costanza, Michael Dukakis, Dianne Feinstein, Ed Koch, Kate Millett, David B. Mixner, Sean Strub, Bruce Voeller, Sarah Weddington, Kay Whitlock, and Molly Yard. Personal papers contain awards, school papers, yearbooks, clippings, and interviews, in addition to pinback buttons, t-shirts, and other memorabilia commemorating gay rights milestones and political campaigns. Photographs feature O'Leary, Bella Abzug, Virginia Apuzzo, Tammy Baldwin, Costanza, Martin Duberman, Florynce Kennedy, Gloria Steinem, and Voeller, and events such as the Lesbian Feminist Liberation "Lavender Dinosaur Demonstration" in New York, Equal Rights Amendment march on Washington D.C., and the Democratic National Convention. Computer and audiovisual media chiefly relate to political campaigns.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jean O'Leary Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Access Now for Gay and Lesbian Equality
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force (U.S.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence, financial and legal records, reports, memoranda, notes, ephemera, clippings, and other papers related to Jean O'Leary's work as an LGBTQ advocate and Democratic political operative. Included are files for O'Leary & Associates, National Gay Rights Advocates, National Gay and Lesbian Task Force, National Coming Out Day, Access Now for Gay and Lesbian Equality, and the Democratic National Committee. The series also contains writings and speeches by O'Leary and materials from conferences and speaking engagements.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: National Gay Rights Advocates, National Gay & Lesbian Task Force, Politics, Businesses and Advocacy Organizations, Speaking Engagements and Conferences, and Writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries documents O'Leary's leadership of and resignation from National Gay Rights Advocates (NGRA). Materials relate to board meetings, budgets, fundraising, publications, and a class action lawsuit brought by NGRA against the National Institutes of Health and Food and Drug Administration over approval times of AIDS drugs. Also included is correspondence with feminist political activists Molly Yard and Lael Stegall, Ohio Governor Richard F. Celeste, New York Bishop Paul Moore, Jr., performers and longtime couple Gean Harwood and Bruhs Mero, and James T. Flanagan, a former director of Services & Advocacy for GLBT Elders.

did.unittitle: Class action suit - NGRA vs. NIH & FDA
Personnel - Staff resignations, firings, and accompanying material
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains administrative files and printed material related to O'Leary's work on the National Gay and Lesbian Task Force.

did.unittitle: Media manual for gay organizations - Draft

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries primarily contains materials related to O'Leary's work as chair of the Democratic National Committee's Lesbian and Gay Caucus and appointment as a delegate to the Democratic National Convention, as well as the inauguration of President Bill Clinton and other political campaigns. Included are letters to O'Leary from Clinton, Ron Brown, David B. Mixner, Michael Dukakis, Dianne Feinstein, Donald L. Fowler, Art Torres, Mark Spengler, and Jeffrey B. Trammell.

did.unittitle: "The Life of the Party" (House of Blues Event)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains materials related to O'Leary's direct marketing firm, O'Leary & Associates, and her work with National Coming Out Day, Access Now for Gay and Lesbian Equality (ANGLE), Gay Activists Alliance, and Gay & Lesbian Democrats of America.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: New Pacific Academy & Critical Literacy Institute
Confide Community Advisory Committee - Direct Access Diagnostics

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains speeches by O'Leary on topics including National Coming Out Day, AIDS treatment, and advocacy, business, politics, and civil rights in the LGBTQ community, as well as materials related to speaking engagements at colleges and panels, conferences, and other events.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: "David McWhirter - Your Life and Times" (San Diego)
First National Gay Business Convention (San Francisco)
"The Gay & Lesbian Community - We Do Have a Voice"
Harvard University - Kennedy School of Government
"Lesbian Feminism - The Building of a New Society"
"Visibility as Power for Lesbians" - National Lesbian Conference - Keynote (San Diego)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains writings by O'Leary that appeared in books and magazines, including "God was an Innocent Bystander" and "The Ex-Nun."

did.unittitle: "From Agitator to Insider: Fighting for Inclusion in the Democratic Party"
"Gay-Rights Law: Confusion and Contradictions" (with Bruce Voeller)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of letters, cards, and invitations to Jean O'Leary. Correspondents include Midge Costanza, feminist writer Kate Millett, authors and gay rights activists Kay Whitlock and David B. Mixner, Roe v. Wade attorney Sarah Weddington, AIDS researcher Bruce Voeller, politicians such as Harry Britt, Ed Koch, Bill Clinton, Al Gore, and Dianne Feinstein, Lambda Legal Defense & Education Fund, Senior Action in a Gay Environment (now Services & Advocacy for GLBT Elders), Gay Services of Kansas, United States Commission on Civil Rights, and Redbook magazine.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains address books, awards, certificates, clippings, interviews, notes, ephemera, and subject files on Midge Costanza, philanthropist Randy Klose, journalist and National Gay Rights Advocates co-founder Richard Rouilard, and gay rights activist and publisher Sean Strub. Also included is memorabilia such as pinback buttons, t-shirts, and sweatshirts commemorating National Coming Out Day, the Gay Games, Democratic political campaigns, and the Democratic National Convention; several rings; and a paper fan with an American flag design.

did.unittitle: California State Board of Equalization award
Gay & Lesbian Pride Month, 1999 Proclamation (William Clinton)
Lambda Legal Defense & Education Fund - Certificate of Appreciation
Franklin & Eleanor Roosevelt Democrat of the Year
Franklin & Eleanor Roosevelt Democrat of the Year
Franklin & Eleanor Roosevelt Democrat of the Year
Los Angeles County Democratic Party - Fundraising Award
Los Angeles County - National Coming Out Day - Award
Los Angeles Gay & Lesbian Center Award
Los Angeles Lesbian Community - Purple Heart Award
National Association of Business Councils - Certificate of Honor
NGTF Fund for Human Dignity - Certificate of Recognition
State of California - Certificate of Alternate Delegate
State of NY Division of Human Rights - Appointment as Member of the Regional Advisory Council, 1975-1976
Stonewall Democratic Club - 1994 Public Official Award
Eagle pin with pearl (gift of Jane Harmon)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains photographs of Jean O'Leary at rallies, marches, ceremonies, and other events, such as the Lesbian Feminist Liberation "Lavender Dinosaur Demonstration" in New York, Equal Rights Amendment march on Washington D.C., and the Democratic National Convention, as well as an appearance with Martin Duberman on the Today Show. Also included are images of Bella Abzug, Virginia Apuzzo, Tammy Baldwin, Ron Brown, Bill Clinton, Midge Costanza, Ginny Foat, Jane Fonda, Al Gore, Barney Frank, Florynce Kennedy, Ian McKellen, Ann Richards, Gloria Steinem, and Bruce Voeller.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials maintain order found at time of acquisition.

did.unittitle: Dinner honoring Jean O'Leary & Bruce Voeller
Jean O'Leary assault case against Julia (Julio) Maudlin
National Gay and Lesbian Task Force retreat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains a Sharp Wizard 512KBhandheld electronic organizer and videocassette tapes related to political campaigns.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Computer Media and Audiovisual Media.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains videocassette tapes of interviews and other recordings related to political campaigns and activism, and one cassette tape.

accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
